description: NPC dialogue lines for MinPlayableMap — survival-focused prehistoric world

voice_assets.text: Stay low. The T-Rex hunts by movement. If you freeze — it passes. If you run — you die. The tribe learned this the hard way. Three hunters gone. Now we know.
The valley belongs to the herd. Forty beasts, each the size of three huts. They eat only leaves — but their feet crush stone. Do not stand between them and the river. Ever.
We did not always fear the night. Before the great lizards came north, we slept under open sky. Now we dig deep. We build walls of stone. The old ones say this will pass. The old ones are wrong.
voice_assets.context: Player discovers the tribe's cave shelter

dialogue_triggers.label: Trigger_TRexTerritory_001
dialogue_triggers.note: Ambient voice — no NPC visible, memory/flashback delivery

npc_roster.label: NPC_Elder_001
npc_roster.name: Mara
npc_roster.personality: Experienced survivor. Speaks in short, direct observations. Has seen three generations of hunters die.

npc_roster.label: NPC_Hunter_001
npc_roster.name: Kael
npc_roster.personality: Pragmatic. Respects competence over seniority. Will test the player before sharing information.

npc_roster.label: NPC_Hunter_002
npc_roster.name: Sora
npc_roster.personality: Young, fast, nervous. Has seen things the others haven't. Knows the herd migration routes.

npc_roster.label: NPC_Scout_001
npc_roster.name: Davan
npc_roster.personality: Calm under pressure. Stationed at the river crossing — critical supply route.

npc_roster.label: NPC_Survivor_001
npc_roster.name: Unknown Survivor
npc_roster.personality: Barely alive. Found in T-Rex territory. Carries information about what lies beyond the valley.

ambient_dialogue_lines.text: Blood on the trail. Fresh. Something was dragged north.
Fires low tonight. The big ones see fire. They remember it.
Rain covers sound. Good for moving. Bad for hearing what follows you.
Ground's shaking. The long-necks are moving. Half a day's walk — maybe less.
Another one gone. We do not mourn long here. Mourning is a luxury. Survival is not.

act_1.title: Alone in the Valley
act_1.description: Player arrives with nothing. Must find food, water, shelter before nightfall. First contact with tribe is hostile — they don't trust outsiders.
key_beats: Player finds first food source (berries, small game)
Player witnesses dinosaur kill from safe distance
Player discovers tribe's cave — barred from entry
Player completes first task for Hunter Kael to earn basic trust

act_2.title: Earning a Place
act_2.description: Player proves competence through hunting, scouting, and resource gathering. Tribe gradually accepts them. Dinosaur threat escalates.
key_beats: Rescue of wounded survivor from T-Rex territory
Discovery of T-Rex nest — threat level rises

act_3.title: The Valley Cannot Hold
act_3.description: T-Rex territory expands. Tribe must decide: defend the cave or migrate. Player's choices determine tribe survival.
key_beats: T-Rex attacks camp for first time
Elder Mara reveals the migration route
Outcome varies by player choices and skill

world_lore.geography: A deep valley carved by an ancient river. High walls on north and south. River runs east-west. T-Rex territory in the northeast. Brachiosaurus herd grazes the western plains.
world_lore.tribe_history: Arrived in the valley 4 seasons ago, fleeing drought to the south. Lost 8 members to dinosaur attacks in the first season. Now know the rules — but the rules keep changing.

dinosaur_ecology.TRex: Solitary. Territorial. Hunts by movement detection. Nest in northeast rock formations. Active dawn and dusk.
dinosaur_ecology.Raptors: Pack hunters. 3-7 individuals. Intelligent. Test prey before committing. Can be driven off with fire.
dinosaur_ecology.Brachiosaurus: Herd of ~40. Passive unless cornered. Migration follows river. Stampede kills more tribe members than predators.
dinosaur_ecology.Other: Smaller herbivores provide reliable food source. Pterosaurs steal cached food. River holds large aquatic predators.
